techniques.name: Passkey Authentication
techniques.description: Passkey authentication is a public-key–based authentication technique standardized by FIDO2/WebAuthn. A unique cryptographic key pair is generated per service. The private key is securely stored on the user’s device and unlocked locally using biometrics or a device PIN. Authentication is performed by signing a server challenge.
sources: @techreport{webauthn2_w3c_2021,  title        = {Web Authentication: An API for accessing Public Key Credentials -- Level 2},  author       = {Hodges, Jeff and Jones, J.C. and Jones, Michael B. and Kumar, Akshay and Lundberg, Emil},  institution  = {World Wide Web Consortium (W3C)},  type         = {W3C Recommendation},  number       = {REC-webauthn-2-20210408},  year         = {2021},  month        = apr,  day          = {8},  url          = {https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn-2/},  note         = {Accessed: 2026-01-05}}
